raw_consumer_statement:
Barclays did not turn off my autopay after requesting it multiple times, then they attempted process a payment every month for 3 years and charging me returned check fee plus interest and late fees excessively. They refuse to file a claim on my behalf and t heir executive office never took action for years now.

Barclays is in violation of the following laws : Electronic Fund Transfer Act ( Regulation E ). I told them to cancel autopay and they kept trying to pull from your bank anyway, thats an unauthorized preauthorized transfer. Reg E requires written authorization for recurring debits and says consumers can revoke and place a stop at least 3 business days before the next debit. Keep trying after revocation can violate 1005.10.

Truth in Lending Act / Fair Credit Billing Act ( Regulation Z ). Stacking returned-payment fees, interest, and late fees that stem from their own autopay error can be a billing error. I have the right to dispute, and the issuer must investigate and correct finance charges/fees caused by their mistake. See 15 U.S.C. 1666 and Reg Z 1026.13.

CARD Act penalty-fee limits ( Reg Z ). Card penalty fees ( late, returned payment, etc. ) must be reasonable and proportional to the violation.
